title: Write to Know: On the Feasibility of Wrist Motion based User-Authentication from Handwriting
venue: Conference on Security and Privacy in Wireless and Mobile Networks (WiSec ’21)

method.input_representation: Raw sensor data, temporal features (mean, std, variance, peak values), frequency features (FFT, PSD, DCT)

evaluation.context: Achieved by M01 (SVM) in the pencil-writing scenario

evaluation_protocol: Per-participant evaluation: target participant data labeled as authentic, others as impostors. Results averaged across all participants.

reported_limitations: High EER compared to static biometrics
Performance degradation at lower sampling frequencies

usability.usability_issues: High battery drain for high-frequency sampling; long enrollment time may discourage users.

key_contribution: Comparative analysis of four state-of-the-art handwriting authentication frameworks on realistic, unconstrained data
Evaluation across three modalities: pencil, finger, and air writing
main_strength: Comprehensive testing of environmental noise and sampling frequency impact
Equitable comparison using a single unified dataset
main_weakness: Lack of a dedicated liveness detection mechanism
insights.notes_for_review: The paper demonstrates that while M01 is promising, there is a significant gap between motion-based biometrics and mainstream static biometrics like fingerprints.
